MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication And Joinder In Co-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Americas Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Isabelle Tan of the Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman, Tel. No. 1-415-545-8608
appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Felicia Jafferies, Tel. No. 1-415-913-4936 appearing for the Defendant CCSF;
Atty. Christoper T. Olsen of Clinton & Clinton, Tel. No. 1-562-216-5000 appearing
for the Defendant ThyssenKrupp Elevator Corporation.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ,Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication And Joinder In Co-Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator Americas Motion For Summary Judgment - Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for premises liability and negligence and granted as to the third cause of action for products liability. Plaintiff Sussan Pourjavid-Granfors' objections to the declaration of Jorge Contreras are sustained. CCSF has not satisfied its summary motion burden as to either of the first two causes of action. CCSF's argument that it was not responsibe for maintaining the elevator is contrary to the legal rule that a principal who personally is not at fault is nonetheless liable for the torts of its agents committed within the course and scope of the agency. (Peredia v. HR Mobile Services, Inc. (2018) 25 Cal. App. 5th 680, 691-92). CCSF's argument that it lacked actual or constructive notice of the escalator being in a dangerous condition is based on inadmissible statements in Mr. Contreras' declaration. That declaration is largely based on information and belief, which is insufficient to show either personal knowledge or a required foundation. Paragraphs 5 and 6 of that declaration, which discuss Mr. Contreras' review of the work orders, are inadmissible per Lane v. City of Sacramento (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1345. CCSF has not presented any evidence regarding how the work orders are created and maintained and thus there is no basis to infer that they constitute a complete and accurate record of the problems with the escalator. CCSF also cites plaintiff's factually devoid response to special interrogatory 2. That interrogatory asks: "Please state in detail any and all facts that YOU believe support the allegations made in YOUR Complaint." While plaintiff did provide a factually devoid response, the court cannot infer based on that one general question that plaintiff cannot prove her case. The cited discovery is not sufficiently comprehensive to make that inference. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 107.) CCSF is entitled to summary adjudication on the products liability claim. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that CCSF did not design, distribute, or manufacture the escalator. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant ThyssenKrupp Elevator Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for premises liability and negligence and granted as to the third cause of action for products liability. Plaintiff Sussan Pourjavid-Granfors' objections to the declaration of Jorge Contreras are sustained. Mr. Contreras' statement that TKE did not control the premises is based on information and belief, which is insufficient to show either personal knowledge or a required foundation. In addition, his deposition states that TKE had the exclusive right to maintain the escalator. TKE's argument that it lacked actual or constructive notice of any defect in the escalator also fails because that argument is also based on inadmissible statements in Mr. Contreras' declaration. Paragraphs 5 and 6 of that declaration, which discuss his review of the work orders, are inadmissible per Lane v. City of Sacramento (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1345. TKE has not provided any evidence regarding how the work orders are created and maintained and no basis to infer that they constitute a complete and accurate record of the problems with the escalator. TKE fails to satisfy its summary motion burden based on plaintiff's discovery responses. TKE cites plaintiff's factually devoid response to special interrogatory 2. That interrogatory asks: "Please state in detail any and all facts that YOU believe support the allegations made in YOUR Complaint." The court cannot infer based on that one general question that plaintiff cannot prove her case. The cited discovery is not sufficiently comprehensive to make that inference. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 107.) In addition, CCSF propounded that interrogatory, so the response is even more attenuated as to TKE. TKE is entitled to summary adjudication on the products liability claim. KONE manufactured the escalator and plaintiff admits that all that TKE has done is provide replacement parts. RULING: The tentative ruling was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S (Re-Noticed) Motion To Enforce Courts Order And For An Order Granting Reimbursement Of Defense Costs.

Cross-defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to enforce court's order and for an order granting reimbursement of defense costs is DENIED in its entirety without prejudice to the City seeking a severable judgment or injunctive relief. While the City disclaims any intent to seek a mandatory injunction or a final judgment, the City does not identify, nor is the court aware of, any procedure where a summary adjudication order can be converted into an immediately enforceable order that does not involve a severable judgment or injunctive relief. The summary adjudication order is an interim order that is not in and of itself enforceable, but will become enforceable once it is included in a judgment.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fifth Cause Of Action (Declaratory Relief Duty To Defend) Against Cross Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America
Off Calendar Aug-29-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Erica De La Sierra, Esq. (415-913-4902) of LeClair Ryan for CROSS-COMPLAINANT/Moving party CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
Mark Brueggemann, Esq. (562-216-5000) of Clinton & Clinton for Defendant/Opposing party Thyssenkripp Elevator through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court raises issues on the motion. Defendant argues Duty to Defend.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT/DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fifth Cause Of Action (Declaratory Relief Duty To Defend) Against Cross Defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America

Cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication on its fifth cause of action for declaratory relief for a duty to defend against cross-defendant Thyssenkrupp Elevator America is granted. Based on the undisputed facts, the City is entitled to a declaration that Thyssenkrupp is obligated to defend it against the claims alleged by plaintiff Sussan Pourjavid-Granfors in this case. Per the duty to defend language in the parties' contract and Crawford Weather Shield Manufacturing, Inc. (2008) 44 Cal. 44 541, Thyssenkrupp became obligated to defend the City against the claims alleged by Ms. Pourjavid-Granfors in this case once the City made a demand that it be defended. All of Thyssenkrupp's procedural arguments lack merit. Thyssenkrupp's argument that it is not obligated to defend the City until there is a determination that it acted negligently is contrary to both the language of the parties' agreement and Crawford. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
